dailyTasks.motivation: Kick‚Äëoff ‚Äì why Python matters for AI agents and how it fits with your MERN background

tasks.title: Install Python‚ÄØ3.11, VS‚ÄØCode (or PyCharm), and Git. Verify with `python --version` and `pip --version`. Add the VS‚ÄØCode Python extension.

tasks.description: Learn the fundamentals of the language syntax
tasks.title: Read & code along ‚Äì ‚ÄúPython Crash Course ‚Äì Chapter‚ÄØ1‚Äë3‚Äù (free on‚ÄØautomatetheboringstuff.com) or the first three sections of the official Python tutorial (docs.python.org/3/tutorial/).
Exercise:‚ÄØa) Print ‚ÄúHello, Agent!‚Äù‚ÄØb) Simple calculator (add, subtract, multiply, divide) using `input()` and `float()`.
Create a one‚Äëpage mind‚Äëmap of today‚Äôs concepts (variables, data types, basic I/O, control flow).

dailyTasks.motivation: From scripts to reusable code ‚Äì functions and modules

tasks.description: Watch a short video on functions (Corey Schafer ‚Äì ‚ÄúPython Functions‚Äù)
tasks.title: Video (15‚ÄØmin) + notes

tasks.description: Read & practice: Functions, arguments, return values, default parameters, `*args`/`**kwargs` (Python Docs ‚Äì Defining Functions)
tasks.title: Write 3 functions: a) factorial, b) palindrome checker, c) temperature converter
Create a folder `utils/` with `__init__.py`. Put the three functions there and import them in a new script `main.py`. Run the script.
Using only `input()` and the functions you just wrote, build a simple command‚Äëline todo app (add, list, remove).

dailyTasks.motivation: Data structures ‚Äì the building blocks for AI agents

tasks.description: Read: Lists, tuples, sets, dicts (Python Docs ‚Äì Data Structures)
tasks.title: Take note of mutability, common methods, and when to use each.
Write a script that: a) reads a CSV line into a list, b) removes duplicates with a set, c) counts word frequencies with a dict, d) stores a fixed‚Äësize configuration in a tuple.
Practice list/dict/set comprehensions and functions like `map`, `filter`, `sorted`.
Read a text file, compute the 10 most common words, and output JSON. Use `collections.Counter` as a bonus.

dailyTasks.motivation: Control flow, error handling & testing ‚Äì write robust agent code

tasks.title: Write a script that simulates a simple state‚Äëmachine (e.g., traffic‚Äëlight controller).
Study `try/except/else/finally`. Write a wrapper function that safely reads a file and logs errors to `stderr`.
Install `pytest` (`pip install pytest`). Write unit tests for the three functions from Day‚ÄØ2 (factorial, palindrome, converter). Run `pytest -q`.
Expand the Day‚ÄØ1 calculator: add input validation, custom exceptions, and unit tests. Keep the code in a package `calc/`.

dailyTasks.motivation: Getting data ‚Äì HTTP, JSON, and basic web‚Äëscraping (your MERN experience will help)

tasks.description: Read: `requests` library basics (official docs) and JSON handling (`json` module).
tasks.title: Write a script that calls the OpenWeatherMap API (free tier) and prints current temperature for a city you choose.
Parse a simple static page (e.g., Wikipedia‚Äôs ‚ÄúPython (programming language)‚Äù page) and extract the first paragraph.
Create a small Python package `weather_agent/` with a function `get_weather(city:str) -> dict`. Include error handling and a docstring.
Add a `README.md` describing how to install (`pip install -r requirements.txt`) and use the package.

dailyTasks.motivation: First taste of AI ‚Äì using a pre‚Äëbuilt language model via an API

tasks.description: Sign up for OpenAI‚Äôs free‚Äëtier API (or use HuggingFace Inference API).
tasks.title: Store the API key in an `.env` file and load it with `python-dotenv`.

tasks.description: Read: Making POST requests with JSON payloads (OpenAI API quick‚Äëstart).
tasks.title: Write a function `ask(prompt:str) -> str` that sends the prompt to `gpt‚Äë3.5‚Äëturbo` and returns the reply.
Wrap the call in a `try/except` block; implement exponential back‚Äëoff if you hit a rate limit.

Combine everything:  
1. Prompt the model: *‚ÄúGive me three tasks I should do today to improve my Python skills.‚Äù*  
2. Parse the JSON‚Äëlike response into a Python list.  
3. Save the list to a local `tasks.json`.  
4. Provide a CLI to mark tasks as done (update the JSON).

dailyTasks.motivation: Consolidate, showcase, and plan next steps

tasks.title: Add type hints, a `setup.py` (or `pyproject.toml`), and a `requirements.txt`. Write docstrings and a short usage guide.
Create a GitHub repo, write a concise `README.md` with screenshots, and push the code. Add the repo to your portfolio site (the same way you showcase MERN projects).

Complete a checklist:  
- Variables, types, control flow ‚úÖ  
- Functions & modules ‚úÖ  
- Data structures ‚úÖ  
- Exceptions & testing ‚úÖ  
- HTTP/JSON ‚úÖ  
- Simple LLM call ‚úÖ  
Identify any gaps and note resources for deeper study (e.g., *Automate the Boring Stuff*, *Python Crash Course*, *Effective Python*).

1Ô∏è‚É£ Week‚ÄØ8‚Äë9 ‚Äì Pandas & NumPy (data manipulation).  
2Ô∏è‚É£ Week‚ÄØ10 ‚Äì Intro to machine‚Äëlearning with scikit‚Äëlearn.  
3Ô∏è‚É£ Week‚ÄØ11 ‚Äì Building a custom LangChain agent.  
4Ô∏è‚É£ Week‚ÄØ12 ‚Äì Deploying the agent on Docker/Heroku and connecting it to a MERN front‚Äëend.
Write these goals in a Notion page or Google Doc and set weekly review reminders.

description: A 7‚Äëday, 3‚Äëhours‚Äëper‚Äëday learning‚Äëby‚Äëdoing plan that leverages your existing MERN skills and delivers a tangible Python‚Äëbased AI‚Äëagent prototype by the end of the week. Each day mixes short theory, hands‚Äëon coding, a mini‚Äëproject, and a brief reflection so you build confidence quickly and have concrete portfolio pieces ready to show.

finalChallenges.description: Deploy the "Agentic Scheduler" to a free cloud platform (Render, Railway, or Fly.io). Use a `Procfile` and expose a simple HTTP endpoint that returns the current task list as JSON.
finalChallenges.title: Deployment Sprint

finalChallenges.description: Integrate the Python micro‚Äëservice with a tiny React front‚Äëend (you already know MERN). The front‚Äëend should call the `/tasks` endpoint, display tasks, and allow marking them complete via a POST request to the Python API.
finalChallenges.title: Full‚ÄëStack Fusion

finalGoal: By Day‚ÄØ7 you will be able to write clean Python code, consume web APIs, handle errors, test your functions, and build a minimal AI‚Äëaugmented agent that you can showcase alongside your MERN portfolio.
name: 7‚ÄëDay Python Boot‚ÄëCamp for Agentic AI (3‚ÄØh/day)
